name: David Bowie
bio: David Bowie's catalog has unusual long-tail value because it spans multiple eras, multiple audience generations, and constant sync-ready cultural reuse.
notable_for: David Bowie remains closely associated with Heroes and Starman, which continue to anchor catalog attention.
career_highlight: Songs like Heroes and Starman still help define the catalog's long-tail earnings profile.
revenue_drivers: Repeat streaming and playlist familiarity help the strongest songs keep earning after release.
Broad catalog recognition improves resilience across radio memory, social reuse, and rediscovery.
Licensing and seasonal or event-driven playback can create recurring revenue spikes.

earnings.assumptions: Estimate keeps David Bowie's current headline range as the artist-side figure and models gross catalog, label, publishing, and writer lanes from that conservative annual range.

ownership.master_owner: Likely split across label, distributor, and artist-affiliated rights depending on recording era
ownership.publishing_owner: Writer and publisher splits materially affect final artist-side income
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No full catalog sale assumption baked into this modeled range
ownership.notes: David Bowie's page should be read as modeled artist-side annual income, not a public royalty statement. Ownership and label terms can materially change take-home economics.
